finding: The current auto-batch rollout analyzer can classify a run as improved from synthetic broker-ticket events when batchRate is at least 0.70 and build/projection ticket counts are within limits, without requiring paired serial/treatment makespan, throughput, cost, or real lane evidence. ATM-GOV-0179's focused test exercises this synthetic path rather than a real paired A/B dogfood run.
expectedBehavior: An improved verdict must require real plan-run control and treatment samples joined through durable task, wave, broker, checkpoint, and lane-session evidence. Missing makespan, throughput, cost, sample-size, or safety evidence must produce inconclusive, never improved.
evidenceOrRepro: In scripts/analyze-captain-parallel-ledger.ts, summarizeAutoBatchPipeline derives improved from ticket batch eligibility and generated-write counts. tests/cli/strict-paired-ab-dogfood.test.ts supplies three synthetic broker-ticket lane events and expects improved without a serial/treatment timing comparison. ATM-GOV-0179 remains historically done, but its performance claim is unproven.
followUp: ATM-GOV-0190 in doc_atm_gov_auto_batch_perf_plan_v2 must deliver Analyzer v3, real lane-session joins, deterministic replay, prospective paired GOV dogfood, sample thresholds, and a verdict that gates on speed, cost, safety, and batching evidence.
